# Poly

> Poly is a cross-platform CLI package manager: one command (`poly install <name>`) that
> routes to pip, npm, Homebrew, cargo, go install, or its own checksum-verified binary
> "tap" downloads, whichever actually has the package, on macOS, Linux, and Windows.
> Poly works alongside pip/npm/brew rather than replacing them ‚Äî it unifies them behind
> a single interface, keeps one local record (`~/.poly/manifest.json`) of what got
> installed through which, and can write that state to a committable `poly.json` so a
> second machine can reproduce it with a single `poly install`.

Poly is free and open source. A one-time ‚Ç¨0.99 "Pro" unlock adds concurrent multi-package
installs and automatic background package upgrades ‚Äî it does not gate any package or
formula; the entire built-in tap catalog is free.

## Install

- macOS installer (.pkg, universal Intel+Apple Silicon): https://github.com/opencorp2030-ctrl/poly/releases/latest/download/poly-macos.pkg
- Windows installer (.exe, installs to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Poly, no admin needed): https://github.com/opencorp2030-ctrl/poly/releases/latest/download/poly-setup.exe
- All prebuilt binaries and release notes: https://github.com/opencorp2030-ctrl/poly/releases/latest
- Build from source (any OS, Go 1.21+): `git clone https://github.com/opencorp2030-ctrl/poly.git && cd poly && go build -o poly .`

## Commands

- `poly install [[adapter:]name[@version] ...]` ‚Äî install packages, auto-routed or forced with a prefix like `pip:requests`. Multiple names install sequentially (free) or concurrently (Pro). No arguments reads `./poly.json` and installs everything listed there.
- `poly remove name` ‚Äî uninstall via whichever adapter installed it.
- `poly list` ‚Äî show everything poly has installed, and through which adapter.
- `poly search [adapter:]name` ‚Äî exact-name existence + latest-version check, across every adapter or one forced.
- `poly info [adapter:]name` ‚Äî like search, plus summary, homepage, and local install status.
- `poly init [--force]` ‚Äî write `poly.json` from currently poly-installed packages, version-pinned, for reproducible environments.
- `poly upgrade` ‚Äî check every installed package against its adapter's latest version and reinstall what's outdated.
- `poly self-update` ‚Äî check GitHub releases, download, SHA-256-verify, and atomically replace the running poly binary.
- `poly login` / `poly logout` / `poly account` ‚Äî Supabase-backed account: sign in, sign out, print your email/username/bio/plan.
- `poly send name version path` ‚Äî publish a file or directory to the community registry under your signed-in account. Installable by anyone via `poly install community:name` (never auto-detected, to prevent name-squatting on real packages).
- `poly version` ‚Äî print the build version and, if signed in, your account/plan.
- `poly doctor` ‚Äî check for common setup problems: missing toolchains, an unwritable `~/.poly`, `~/.poly/bin` missing from `PATH`, GitHub reachability.

## Adapters (in auto-detect order)

1. `tap` ‚Äî direct binary download from a pinned URL in an embedded/local YAML formula, SHA-256 verified, extracted into `~/.poly/bin`. No runtime needed.
2. `brew` ‚Äî shells to `brew install`/`brew upgrade`. Search hits the public formulae.brew.sh API.
3. `pip` ‚Äî shells to `pip3 install --upgrade` (or `==version` pinned). Search hits the PyPI JSON API.
4. `npm` ‚Äî shells to `npm install -g name@latest` (or `@version`). Search hits the npm registry API.
5. `cargo` ‚Äî shells to `cargo install --force`. Search hits the crates.io API.
6. `go` ‚Äî shells to `go install <module>@version`; takes a full module import path, not a short name (e.g. `golang.org/x/tools/cmd/goimports`), so it's realistically only used via an explicit `go:` prefix.

`community` is a 7th adapter (user-published packages, via `poly send`) that is deliberately excluded from auto-detect order ‚Äî only reachable via an explicit `community:` prefix, so a published name can never silently shadow a real pip/npm/crates.io package.

## MCP server (for AI assistants)

Poly runs a remote MCP server (Streamable HTTP transport, plus OAuth 2.1 + Dynamic Client
Registration for clients like Claude.ai/ChatGPT that only support OAuth for remote
connectors) at `https://iuymslcbbrbahxbfuzrr.supabase.co/functions/v1/mcp`, hosted as a
Supabase Edge Function, so an AI assistant can act as a signed-in Poly user without ever
handling that user's email/password. Auth is a personal access token generated on
account.html (shown once, revocable any time) -- either sent directly as `Authorization:
Bearer <token>`, or pasted into the OAuth connect page during the "Add custom connector"
flow (the resulting OAuth access_token IS that same personal access token). Tools
exposed: `whoami`, `search_packages`/`search_dependencies` (community registry vs.
pip+npm+crates.io+Homebrew+tap+community all at once), `get_package`, `list_own_packages`,
`publish_package` (base64-encoded file in the tool call args ‚Äî same first-come-first-served
ownership rules and SHA-256 verification as `poly send`; an assistant can never mark a
package "official"; the tool description explicitly warns against building an HTML/web
app -- published packages are CLI scripts/binaries, not web pages), `search_members`,
`get_profile`, `update_profile`, `update_avatar`, `delete_package`, `get_stats`
(`update_profile`/`update_avatar`/`delete_package` act only on the token's own account/
packages; `delete_package` removes both the DB entry and the stored file and cannot be
undone). Deliberately NOT exposed via MCP: creating, listing, or revoking personal access
tokens -- an assistant can use the access it's given but never grant or manage its own.
Full setup instructions, including the Claude Desktop/Code config JSON:
https://poly.candygate.eu/docs.html#mcp

## Key files and formats

- `poly.json` (project-local, written by `poly init`): `{"packages": ["tap:ripgrep@15.1.0", "npm:eslint@9.2.0"]}`. `poly install` with no arguments reads this.
- Tap formula YAML (`~/.poly/taps/<name>.yaml` or embedded in the binary): fields `name`, `description`, `homepage`, `version`, `binary`, `tier` (`free` or `pro`), and `artifacts` keyed by `<GOOS>_<GOARCH>` each with `url` and `sha256`.
- `~/.poly/manifest.json` ‚Äî local record of installed packages.
- `~/.poly/credentials.json` (mode 0600) ‚Äî signed-in session.
- `~/.poly/auto-update.log` ‚Äî output of the background self-update / auto-upgrade checks.

## Site

- Homepage: https://poly.candygate.eu/
- Full docs: https://poly.candygate.eu/docs.html
- Account (sign up / manage plan): https://poly.candygate.eu/account.html
- Community directory + live download/member stats: https://poly.candygate.eu/community.html
- Cross-ecosystem dependency search (pip/npm/crates.io/Homebrew/community, live): https://poly.candygate.eu/dependencies.html
- Public user profiles: https://poly.candygate.eu/profile.html?u=<username>
- Public package detail pages: https://poly.candygate.eu/package.html?name=<package>
- Source code, issues: https://github.com/opencorp2030-ctrl/poly
